questions.question:
The diagram below shows a river system consisting of 7 segments, marked P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V. It splits the land into 5 zones, marked Z1, Z2, Z3, Z4, and Z5. We need to connect these zones using the least number of bridges. Out of the following options, which one is correct?
Note: The figure shown is representative.



(GATE CSE 2025 SET-2)
options:
B.

Bridges on P, Q, S, and T

C.

Bridges on Q, R, T, and V

D.

Bridges on P, Q, S, U, and V
questions.correct_answer:
C.

Bridges on Q, R, T, and V
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which one of the following options is correct for the given data in the table?
\( \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline \text{Iteration} (i) & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline \text{Input} (I) & 20 & -4 & 10 & 15 \\ \hline \text{Output} (X) & 20 & 16 & 26 & 41 \\ \hline \text{Output} (Y) & 20 & -80 & -800 & -12000 \\ \hline \end{array} \)

(GATE CSE 2025 SET-2)
options:
A.

\(X(i) = X(i - 1) + I(i); \quad Y(i) = Y(i - 1) I(i); \quad i > 0 \)

B.

\(X(i) = X(i - 1) I(i); \quad Y(i) = Y(i - 1) + I(i); \quad i > 0 \)

C.

\(X(i) = X(i - 1) I(i); \quad Y(i) = Y(i - 1) I(i); \quad i > 0 \)
questions.correct_answer:
A.

\(X(i) = X(i - 1) + I(i); \quad Y(i) = Y(i - 1) I(i); \quad i > 0 \)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Based only on the conversation below, identify the logically correct inference:

"Even if I had known that you were in the hospital, I would not have gone there to see you", Ramya told Josephine.

(GATE CSE 2025 SET-2)
options:
A.

Ramya knew that Josephine was in the hospital.

B.

Ramya did not know that Josephine was in the hospital.

C.

Ramya and Josephine were once close friends; but now, they are not.

D.

Josephine was in the hospital due to an injury to her leg.
questions.correct_answer:
B.

Ramya did not know that Josephine was in the hospital.

questions.question:
Let \(p_1 \) and \(p_2 \) denote two arbitrary prime numbers. Which one of the following statements is correct for all values of \(p_1 \) and \(p_2 \)?

(GATE CSE 2025 SET-2)
options:
A.

\(p_1 + p_2 \) is not a prime number.

B.

\(p_1 p_2 \) is not a prime number.
questions.correct_answer:
B.

\(p_1 p_2 \) is not a prime number.

questions.question:
"I put the brown paper in my pocket along with the chalks, and possibly other things. I suppose everyone must have reflected how primeval and how poetical are the things that one carries in one's pocket: the pocket-knife, for instance the type of all human tools, the infant of the sword. Once I planned to write a book of poems entirely about the things in my pocket. But I found it would be too long: and the age of the great epics is past."

(From G.K. Chesterton's "A Piece of Chalk")

Based only on the information provided in the above passage, which one of the following statements is true?

(GATE CSE 2025 SET-1)
options:
A.

The author of the passage carries a mirror in his pocket to reflect upon things.

B.

The author of the passage had decided to write a poem on epics.

C.

The pocket-knife is described as the infant of the sword.

D.

Epics are described as too inconvenient to write.
questions.correct_answer:
C.

The pocket-knife is described as the infant of the sword.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
